title: edge temporal representation → INTEGER epoch seconds, with a hard-reject normalisation boundary

commits.summary: 10 failing + 1 already-passing TC-33 tests in tc33_edge_temporal_epoch.rs; malformed-string / non-string-JSON / schema-CHECK / temporal_fallback / positive-normalisation properties

INTEGER epoch storage + governed SDK; schema step 23 recreate with typeof CHECKs; extractor+consolidation hard-reject normalisation boundary; edge read path unified with node :now seam; bindings + interface docs; all pre-existing tests updated

gates.note: 0 warnings, 0 errors. One initial clippy::type_complexity warning was resolved with a local `type EdgeTimes` alias.
13 ignored includes eu7_real_corpus_ac (#[ignore]d, R-20-EU7 CLOSED BY HITL — left untouched, zero eu7 runs).

tc33_representation_as_built.storage: canonical_edges.t_valid / t_invalid are INTEGER epoch seconds (UTC); NULL = still valid
tc33_representation_as_built.schema_step: 23 (SCHEMA_VERSION 22 → 23); recreate canonical_edges with `INTEGER ... CHECK (x IS NULL OR typeof(x) = 'integer')` on both columns
tc33_representation_as_built.governed_sdk_surface: PreparedWrite::Edge.t_valid/t_invalid and ConsolidateCandidateEdge.t_valid/t_invalid are Option<i64>; py dict_epoch_seconds, napi json_i64_alt
tc33_representation_as_built.extractor_wire: fathomdb.extract.v1 keeps ISO-8601 strings; engine-side normalize_extractor_timestamp() converts to epoch with hard rejection
tc33_representation_as_built.consolidation_wire: ISO-8601 both directions — epoch_seconds_to_iso8601() outbound, normalize_extractor_timestamp() inbound on the invalidate verdict's t_invalid
tc33_representation_as_built.not_not_null: the structural spelling is a typeof CHECK, NOT `NOT NULL`, so NULL-means-still-valid survives (asserted by tc33_p4_null_timestamp_remains_storable_and_means_still_valid)

fail_loud_evidence.red_at_bbf305a0: malformed strings ('not a date', 'yesterday sometime', '') each ingested successfully and wrote 1 edge; JSON-number/bool/object t_valid/t_invalid each ingested and wrote 1 edge (the pre-existing as_str()->None->NULL coercion); P3 InvalidColumnType(0,'t_valid',Text); P4 raw INSERT of 'not a date' succeeded and UPDATE overwriting a past t_invalid with junk succeeded (the resurrection vector); P5 InvalidColumnType(1,'t_valid',Text).
fail_loud_evidence.green_at_a61f3272: all 11 pass. A malformed/non-string extractor timestamp now returns EngineError::InvalidArgument naming t_valid/t_invalid and writes ZERO edge rows (asserted by assert_hard_reject's edge_count == 0). The schema CHECK rejects a raw TEXT INSERT as a constraint violation, and rejects an UPDATE that would overwrite a past integer t_invalid with junk — the invalidation survives as integer 1577836800 (tc33_p4_junk_cannot_resurrect_an_invalidated_edge).
fail_loud_evidence.polarity: junk NEVER becomes NULL t_invalid: it is rejected at the write boundary AND is structurally unstorable, so an invalidated edge cannot be silently resurrected.

temporal_fallback_regrounding.problem: match was a raw byte-for-byte string compare of edge t_valid against the free-form ELPS warnings key substituted_t_valid, with .unwrap_or(false) on the miss path. Normalising only the edge side would leave the set never matching, silently turning every fallback edge into a trusted edge — no compile error.
temporal_fallback_regrounding.fix: both the edge t_valid/t_invalid AND every substituted_t_valid warning are normalised through normalize_extractor_timestamp() in ONE pass under one connection lock BEFORE the edges are built; the fallback set is HashSet<i64> of epochs and the match is is_some_and(|tv| fallback_epochs.contains(&tv)). A malformed substituted_t_valid REJECTS (not skips) — skipping would leave the edge unflagged = treated as trusted, the same fail-open elsewhere.
temporal_fallback_regrounding.bonus_fix: '2025-03-20T09:30:00Z' and '2025-03-20T09:30:00+00:00' are the same instant but missed each other under the old byte compare; as epochs they match. Proven by tc33_p5 (fixture spells the two sides differently).
temporal_fallback_regrounding.guarded_by: tc33_p5_temporal_fallback_still_flags_after_normalisation; the flag still gates slice30_graph_arm.rs and pr_c1_graph_seeding.rs (both GREEN).

preexisting_tests_changed.change: t_invalid_tformat_edge_correctly_excluded RENAMED expired_edge_is_excluded_from_traversal; its old docstring (T-vs-space separator broke the raw TEXT comparison) preserved as evidence the TEXT representation already bit once. Under INTEGER the representation-level property is vacuous; kept as the traversal-exclusion assertion. edge_with_t_invalid helper now takes i64.
preexisting_tests_changed.why: TC-33 removes the representation the test existed to guard.

preexisting_tests_changed.change: 't_valid must be preserved from extract response' → asserts normalised equivalence (ISO 2020-01-01T00:00:00Z on the wire → epoch 1577836800 in storage); owns_row/works_row column types String→i64.
preexisting_tests_changed.why: 'preserved' is the exact contract TC-33 changes; the extractor wire stays ISO but the engine normalises it.

preexisting_tests_changed.change: s22_edge_temporal_columns_are_untouched_text RENAMED ..._text_at_v22_become_integer_at_head — pins TEXT at v22 (step 22 is node-only) AND INTEGER at head (step 23 resolves the divergence). Module docstring updated. s22_is_idempotent_across_repeated_migrate_calls now migrates the whole registry to head (v22 is no longer head).
preexisting_tests_changed.why: the divergence step 22 recorded is now resolved, not frozen.

preexisting_tests_changed.change: s14_legacy_rows_null_safe_for_g11_columns scoped to steps 14..=22 (was 14..head) with an explicit comment.
preexisting_tests_changed.why: a pre-G11 edge row cannot survive the step-23 recreate under NO-DATA-MIGRATION; the property under test (step-14 additive nullability) holds for 14..=22.

preexisting_tests_changed.change: head-version pins 22→23, migration step-id lists gain 23, migration count 21→22, compatibility event list gains 23.

edge temporal test fixtures converted from ISO strings to epoch integers; helper signatures String→i64; consolidate_provider gained now_epoch() and integer temporal-liveness probes; edge_row reads t_invalid as Option<i64>. Consolidation VERDICT JSON (harness wire) stays ISO — the engine renders candidates out to ISO and normalises the verdict back.
preexisting_tests_changed.why: governed-write fixtures must carry epoch integers now; extractor/consolidation wire fixtures stay ISO.

preexisting_tests_changed.change: governed-write tInvalid/tValid/t_invalid fixtures converted from ISO strings to epoch integers; helper param types string→number/int. test_option2_elps_pipeline.py:119 ('t_valid' in edge) left as-is — it asserts the EXTRACTOR wire, which stays ISO.
preexisting_tests_changed.why: napi json_i64_alt / py dict_epoch_seconds now reject a string on the governed write surface.
preexisting_tests_changed.caveat: INSPECTED AND UPDATED BY HAND, NOT EXECUTED — the cross-language suites require a native rebuild (maturin/napi), which is forbidden from this worktree. No compile-time protection on the JSON wire.

plan_or_recon_corrections: Recon said schema :630 is the step-22 SQL string with 'which are unchanged' prose; correct. I left the applied SQL text verbatim and corrected the Rust comment at :596-611 plus the step-14 comment, as directed.

NEW HAZARD not in recon: step 23 DROPs canonical_edges, which lowers load_next_cursor's MAX(write_cursor) high-water mark. A reused cursor collides with stale _fathomdb_projection_terminal / _fathomdb_vector_rows / vec0 rows, silently marking a new row as already-projected. Handled by reserving the pre-drop MAX into _fathomdb_open_state (RESERVED_WRITE_CURSOR_KEY) and folding it into load_next_cursor. This is NOT a data migration — it keeps an identifier counter monotonic.

NEW: the reservation INSERT needed `HAVING MAX(write_cursor) IS NOT NULL` — a bare aggregate over an empty canonical_edges returns one NULL row, which violates _fathomdb_open_state.value NOT NULL, i.e. it failed on EVERY fresh DB until fixed (caught by the first GREEN test run, not by review).

NEW: the CONSOLIDATION harness wire is a second ISO-8601 boundary the recon did not call out for the OUTBOUND direction. The reference stub echoes the winner's t_valid straight back as the verdict t_invalid, so candidates had to be rendered epoch→ISO on the way out or our own inbound normaliser would reject the reply. Added epoch_seconds_to_iso8601().

Recon listed 7 comparison sites; there are effectively 8 edge-validity predicates once build_bfs_with_depth_sql (the search_expand template) is counted — recon flagged its sibling :10493 but the depth variant is a separate template that also needed re-grounding. Both were fixed.
The extractor parse site also had a THIRD implicit fail-open surface beyond string/number: a JSON object/array. All non-string, non-null values now hard-reject uniformly via json_type_name().
no_data_migration_compliance: No row-conversion SQL was written. Step 23 is a plain recreate; existing edge rows do not survive; no stored ISO value is converted. The structural-CHECK requirement and the NO-DATA-MIGRATION ruling did NOT conflict — SQLite cannot add a CHECK or change column type in place, so a recreate is required regardless, and the ruling makes it a plain (non-staging) recreate.

invariants_confirmed.inline_clock_rule_engine_9161: honoured — strftime('%s', ?1) is on a BOUND user value, not 'now'; the current instant stays on the bound :now seam. No new datetime('now')/strftime('%s','now') introduced on the edge path.

fix-1.finding: codex §9 P2 CONCERN — the consolidation edges_json render (lib.rs :4792-4795) used `e.t_valid.and_then(|ts| epoch_seconds_to_iso8601(connection, ts))`, so a stored NON-NULL epoch outside SQLite's strftime('%s','unixepoch') renderable range (> 253402300799 = year >= 10000, or before year 0) rendered to a silent `null` on the consolidation wire. A `null` t_invalid reads as "still valid"; the reference stub echoes the winner's t_valid back as the verdict's t_invalid, so the null round-trips through the inbound normaliser as "still valid" — the outbound twin of the fail-open TC-33 removes, resurrecting an invalidated edge via the consolidation path.
fix-1.reachability: The governed integer surface (PreparedWrite::Edge, arbitrary i64) is the ONLY ingress. Inbound ISO normalisation (normalize_extractor_timestamp) can never mint such an epoch — a 4-digit-year ISO string maxes at 9999.
fix-1.governed_surface_status: PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED — no AC minted; tracked by requirement TC-33 + test names.

commits.summary: tests/tc33_fix1_unrenderable_epoch.rs — writes an edge with t_valid/t_invalid = 253402300800 (year 10000, one past the renderable max) through engine.write() and asserts a typed InvalidArgument naming the field + zero rows. Plus a precision guard that the max renderable instant (9999-12-31T23:59:59Z = 253402300799) STAYS storable.
Two layers in fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs. See layers_implemented.

layers_implemented.primary_structural: validate_write's Edge branch (destructure now binds t_valid/t_invalid) calls reject_unrenderable_edge_epoch for each non-null timestamp. The rejection PREDICATE is renderability under epoch_seconds_to_iso8601 itself (SQLite's own strftime — derive-from-SQLite, EXACT, cannot drift), returning EngineError::InvalidArgument naming the offending value and the bound. MIN_RENDERABLE_EPOCH=-62167219200 (0000-01-01T00:00:00Z) / MAX_RENDERABLE_EPOCH=253402300799 (9999-12-31T23:59:59Z) consts carry the year-0000..9999 rationale and are used only to name the bound in the message. Mirrors the inbound normalize_extractor_timestamp hard-reject and the Node branch's valid_from>=valid_until refusal.
layers_implemented.backstop_defensive: The consolidation edges_json render (lib.rs, formerly :4792-4795) closure is now fallible: a local `render(field, Option<i64>)` returns Value::Null for None (unknown, unchanged) but a typed EngineError::InvalidArgument labelled INVARIANT VIOLATION for a Some(ts) that fails to render, collected via `.collect::<Result<Vec<Value>, EngineError>>()?`. Commented as an unreachable hard-assert upholding the invariant structurally, NOT the primary path — the write-boundary guard makes an unrenderable stored epoch impossible.

fix-1.red_evidence: At 56e2eb4a: `cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test tc33_fix1_unrenderable_epoch -- --test-threads=1` → 1 passed; 2 failed. Both failures were `TC-33 fix-1: an unrenderable epoch MUST be rejected at the governed write boundary, but the write succeeded.` (tc33_fix1_unrenderable_t_invalid_is_rejected @ :73:18 and tc33_fix1_unrenderable_t_valid_is_rejected @ :73:18). tc33_fix1_max_renderable_epoch_is_storable already passed (precision guard). Exit 101. Post-fix (39f35108): all 3 pass, exit 0.

gates.note: 571 (pre-fix GREEN) + 3 new fix-1 tests. 13 ignored unchanged (incl. eu7_real_corpus_ac — zero eu7 runs).

fix-1.node_edge_asymmetry_note: canonical_nodes.valid_from/valid_until are ALSO INTEGER epoch and get NO renderable-range guard — deliberately. Nodes are never rendered to ISO for an LLM boundary (grep confirms: no epoch_seconds_to_iso8601 call on the node path; the consolidation render is edge-only), so they lack this outbound fail-open. Per the task's explicit direction, NO node-side guard was added (no real node-timestamp render path exists). The asymmetry is: the guard tracks the RENDER surface, not the storage type — edges render to ISO (consolidation wire) so they are guarded; nodes do not so they are not. If a future slice adds a node→ISO render path, that path needs the same guard.
fix-1.consideration_resolved: reject_unrenderable_edge_epoch could have been made generic over nodes too, but that would widen scope past the finding and guard a non-existent failure mode. Kept edge-only. No ledger TC written (the asymmetry is captured here + in the const/fn doc-comments in code; a ledger entry was judged not warranted since no cross-session ambiguity remains).

fix-2.finding: codex §9 P2 CONCERN — 'The patch leaves at least one retrieval path able to return expired edge bodies, so edge temporal validity is not consistently enforced across search.' TC-33 defines edge_validity_sql (lib.rs :1800-1801) as the ONE shared read predicate ('t_invalid IS NULL OR t_invalid > :now') and already applies it on the graph-traversal, graph-seeding and projection paths — but two ordinary-search paths that hydrate an edge BODY gated on `superseded_at IS NULL` ALONE, so a body-bearing edge written with `t_invalid <= now` (expired/invalidated) still surfaced its body through search(). This is the edge-search twin of the Slice 15b 'validity enforced on traversal, not on search' gap.
fix-2.governed_surface_status: PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED — no AC minted; tracked by requirement TC-33 + test names.

edge_search_paths_inspected.site: lib.rs edge-body FTS branch (was :9855-9861 in the task, ~:9962-:9991 at fix-2 HEAD) — `search_index_edges sei JOIN canonical_edges ce`, appended to text_results as SoftFallbackBranch::TextEdge
edge_search_paths_inspected.pre_fix: WHERE search_index_edges MATCH ?1 AND ce.superseded_at IS NULL — validity NOT applied
edge_search_paths_inspected.disposition: CHANGED — added edge_validity_sql("ce", 2); bound view.edge_now() at ?2 (was single-param [match_expression]). This is the site codex named.

edge_search_paths_inspected.site: lib.rs vector-projected edge-fact hydration `edge_stmt` (~:9636-:9639 pre-fix) — the VECTOR arm hydrates an edge body from canonical_edges by write_cursor when a vector_default row (kind='edge_fact') ranks in the phase-2 pool
edge_search_paths_inspected.pre_fix: WHERE write_cursor = ?1 AND superseded_at IS NULL AND body IS NOT NULL — validity NOT applied
edge_search_paths_inspected.disposition: CHANGED — added edge_validity_sql("canonical_edges", 2); bound view.edge_now() at ?2 (was [rowid]). This is the 'at least one' beyond the named site — the vector-projected edge path the TextEdge doc-comment (:1283-1295) warns edge hits also come from. My RED test exercises BOTH arms (ordinary search() fuses vector + edge-FTS), so both had to be fixed for GREEN.

edge_search_paths_inspected.site: lib.rs graph-seeding edge-fact FTS (~:10253-:10262) — seeds the graph arm from matched edge endpoints
edge_search_paths_inspected.pre_fix: already `AND ce.superseded_at IS NULL{edge_validity_sql("ce",3)} AND temporal_fallback=0`
edge_search_paths_inspected.disposition: LEFT — already validity-filtered (and fallback-filtered). Confirmed, not assumed.

edge_search_paths_inspected.site: lib.rs search_expand_in_tx Step-1 edge existence re-check (~:10987-:10990) — `SELECT 1 FROM canonical_edges WHERE write_cursor=?1 AND superseded_at IS NULL` for a TextEdge hit
edge_search_paths_inspected.disposition: LEFT — deliberately. This path reads NO body (SELECT 1); it only re-confirms an already-surfaced TextEdge hit still exists before skipping BFS, and it keeps the hit's ALREADY-captured body, it does not re-retrieve one. Every TextEdge hit in `search_hits` now comes from the two paths above, both of which filter validity, so an expired edge can no longer ENTER search_hits and therefore can never reach this re-check. Adding edge_validity here would be untested defence-in-depth against a mid-query expiry window; scoped out to avoid changing an un-covered gate. The BFS proper (build_bfs_with_depth_sql, bound at :11046) already carries edge_validity via edge_now.

edge_search_paths_inspected.site: lib.rs assemble_consolidate_cluster (~:4906-:4921) — gathers competing fact-edges for one (subject,relation) axis for consolidation
edge_search_paths_inspected.disposition: LEFT — not a SEARCH retrieval path. Consolidation deliberately reasons over the full active competing set (including expired edges) to decide currency; filtering by validity here would hide the very edges consolidation must adjudicate. Out of the finding's scope (no search-surfacing).

fix-2.now_seam_note: Both fixes bind the frozen `view.edge_now()` (a resolved i64 stored in FrozenView at freeze time) into a positional parameter — never `datetime('now')`/`strftime('%s','now')` (the :9161 no-inline-clock rule). edge_now is ALWAYS present (unlike node validity's Option<i64> now_param, which is None under existence-relaxation), so the edge-validity conjunct is unconditional: an edge invalidated in the past stays excluded even when the caller relaxes NODE existence. This matches the FrozenView::edge_now doc contract and the graph-seed/traversal call sites.
fix-2.byte_stability_note: NODE search hydration (text/BM25F + vector node branch) is byte-unchanged — only the edge-body SELECTs gained a conjunct. On any corpus whose edges never set t_invalid, `t_invalid IS NULL` is TRUE for every edge, so the row-set, bm25 ordering and scores of the edge arm are also unchanged; only edges explicitly invalidated in the past are now dropped.

commits.summary: tests/tc33_fix2_edge_validity_on_search.rs — writes 3 body-bearing edges sharing FTS token 'tc33edgevalidity' (one t_invalid in the PAST, one t_invalid NULL, one t_invalid in the FUTURE), polls ordinary search() until the whole batch is projected AND the valid edge is present, then asserts the expired edge body is ABSENT while both still-valid edge bodies are PRESENT. Asserts on actual search() results.
lib.rs — apply edge_validity_sql on the edge-body FTS branch and the vector-projected edge-fact hydration; bind view.edge_now() at ?2 on both. See edge_search_paths_inspected.

fix-2.red_evidence: At d54ee2cd (RED test on top of unfixed lib.rs — captured by stashing the fix, running the test, then restoring): `cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test tc33_fix2_edge_validity_on_search -- --test-threads=1` → `test result: FAILED. 0 passed; 1 failed`. Panic: `TC-33 fix-2: an EXPIRED edge (t_invalid in the past) must NOT surface its body through ordinary search — edge validity is not enforced on the search read path; got: ["tc33edgevalidity expiredfact alpha", "tc33edgevalidity validnullfact beta", "tc33edgevalidity validfuturefact gamma"]`. Exit 101. Crucially the TWO sibling assertions (validnullfact + validfuturefact must be present) PASSED before the panic — the failure is ONLY the expired-absent assertion, proving the fix is not a vacuous exclude-everything. (The panic line was reported as :130 against the pre-`cargo fmt` test source; the committed test is fmt-normalised — same three assertions, same logic. Post-fix at d0ca54da: `test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed`, exit 0.)

invariants_confirmed.AC-041_recovery_denylist: unchanged — {recover, restore, repair, fix, rebuild}; diff touches only the two edge-search SELECTs and the new test, no recovery/verb code.
invariants_confirmed.node_search_byte_stability: untouched — node text/vector hydration SELECTs are unchanged; only edge-body reads gained the conjunct.
invariants_confirmed.inline_clock_rule_engine_9161: honoured — edge :now is the bound view.edge_now(); no datetime('now')/strftime('%s','now') introduced.

fix-3.finding: codex §9 P2 — 'error-taxonomy regression on malformed consolidation verdicts ... a caller-visible contract break.' At the `invalidate` verdict arm of the consolidation apply loop (lib.rs :4995-5000), the verdict's `t_invalid` is normalised through `normalize_extractor_timestamp`, whose `?` propagated its `EngineError::InvalidArgument { msg }` verbatim for a malformed/non-string timestamp. Consolidation is a BYO-LLM PROVIDER boundary whose documented leaf is `EngineError::Consolidator` ("ConsolidatorError"), so a provider protocol fault (the LLM consolidator emitted a bad t_invalid) was MISCLASSIFIED as a user bad-argument — exactly the bad-timestamp scenario TC-33 hardens. The two SIBLING failure modes on the same value were already correct: missing key → Consolidator (:4998), null/unparseable-to-None → Consolidator (:5000). Only the malformed/non-string case leaked InvalidArgument.
fix-3.governed_surface_status: PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED — no AC minted; tracked by requirement TC-33 + test names. NEVER HITL-SIGNED.
fix-3.site_changed: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs — the `invalidate` verdict arm of the consolidation apply loop (was :4995-5000). Inserted `.map_err(|_| EngineError::Consolidator)` between the normalise call and the trailing `.ok_or(EngineError::Consolidator)?`, with a WHY comment (consolidation is a provider boundary → Consolidator, not the extractor/user InvalidArgument; Consolidator is a unit variant and the adjacent .ok_or cases already discard any message, so this is consistent with the two siblings, not a diagnostic loss).
fix-3.other_consolidation_verdict_timestamp_si: ENUMERATED — grep for normalize_extractor_timestamp found 4 call sites: :4665 / :4670 / :4680 are all on the EXTRACTOR ingest path (raw_edges t_valid/t_invalid and raw_fallback_dates substituted_t_valid); :4995 is the ONLY consolidation-verdict site. The consolidation verdict carries a single timestamp (t_invalid on the invalidate verdict) — no t_valid or second verdict-kind timestamp exists. Only :4995 was changed.
fix-3.extractor_path_untouched: The extractor call sites (:4665-4684) STILL return EngineError::InvalidArgument { msg } naming t_valid/t_invalid — deliberately unchanged so the pinned 15c tests (tc33_edge_temporal_epoch.rs) stay GREEN. Scope guard honoured: consolidation site only.
fix-3.extractor_taxonomy_question_note: Recorded via dev/agent-tools/ledgerwrite as TC-43 (kind=question, status=open, seq 63): the extractor is ALSO a BYO-LLM provider boundary with its own EngineError::Extractor leaf, so a malformed EXTRACTOR timestamp could symmetrically be argued a provider fault (Extractor) not InvalidArgument. A broader taxonomy call for HITL/steward; DEFERRED — changing it would break the pinned 15c tests. NOT resolved here.

commits.summary: tests/tc33_fix3_consolidation_timestamp_taxonomy.rs — drives ordinary consolidate_with_provider() against an inline harness that rules `invalidate` on edge-acme with (a) a malformed STRING t_invalid ('not-a-timestamp') and (b) a non-string JSON number (12345), asserting the result is Err(EngineError::Consolidator) in both. RED against unpatched code (both leaked InvalidArgument).
lib.rs — .map_err(|_| EngineError::Consolidator) on the consolidation invalidate-verdict timestamp normalise; + ledger TC-43. See site_changed.

fix-3.red_evidence: At d32a4496 (`cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test tc33_fix3_consolidation_timestamp_taxonomy -- --test-threads=1`) → `test result: FAILED. 0 passed; 2 failed`, exit 101. malformed_string_t_invalid_is_a_consolidator_fault panicked: `a malformed consolidation t_invalid must return Err(Consolidator) (provider boundary), got Err(InvalidArgument { msg: "extractor edge field `t_invalid` must be an ISO-8601 timestamp SQLite can parse; got \"not-a-timestamp\", which `strftime('%s', ?)` resolves to NULL. REJECTED rather than stored as NULL: a NULL `t_invalid` reads as \"still valid\" and would silently resurrect an invalidated edge. Use JSON null for \"unknown\"." })`. non_string_t_invalid_is_a_consolidator_fault panicked: `... got Err(InvalidArgument { msg: "extractor edge field `t_invalid` must be an ISO-8601 string or JSON null; got a JSON number (12345). ..." })`. Post-fix at 5e52b174: `test result: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed`, exit 0.

invariants_confirmed.governed_surface: PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED; no AC minted; never HITL-SIGNED.
invariants_confirmed.extractor_path_byte_stability: untouched — the extractor normalise sites still return InvalidArgument { msg }; only the consolidation :4995 site remaps to Consolidator.

fix-4.finding: codex §9 P1 — 'The schema migration can leave projection-terminal gaps for dropped edge rows that were pending projection, which can permanently block projection cursor advancement after upgrade.' `write_cursor` is a SINGLE global sequence shared across nodes AND edges; `advance_projection_cursor` (engine) walks the readiness watermark forward ONE value at a time, only while the next cursor has a `_fathomdb_projection_terminal` row. Step 23 (TC-33 canonical_edges recreate) DROPs every edge row. A body-bearing edge whose vector projection had NOT completed at upgrade has NO terminal row; step 23 dropped that row but left its cursor value with no terminal and no owning row, so the projection cursor STALLED PERMANENTLY at that gap — and because the sequence is shared, this froze advancement past SURVIVING node projections too, wedging wait_for_idle / search-freshness on every upgraded DB.
fix-4.not_a_data_migration: This is projection-cursor STATE bookkeeping, NOT data preservation. The NO-DATA-MIGRATION ruling (HITL 2026-07-21) forbids preserving/converting user ROWS; the edge rows still do not survive. The fix only reconciles the engine's internal cursor state machine so it does not dangle on cursors whose rows the recreate correctly dropped.
fix-4.governed_surface_status: PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED — no AC minted; tracked by requirement TC-33 + test names. NEVER HITL-SIGNED.
fix-4.fix: In step 23's SQL string (src/rust/crates/fathomdb-schema/src/lib.rs), BEFORE `DROP TABLE canonical_edges` (while the table still exists to read) and after `DELETE FROM search_index_edges`, inserted: `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO _fathomdb_projection_terminal(write_cursor, state) SELECT write_cursor, 'up_to_date' FROM canonical_edges;`. Sits inside the same step batch, which apply_one wraps with the PRAGMA user_version bump in one BEGIN IMMEDIATE — crash-safety/idempotence unchanged (a crash rolls back to user_version 22 and the whole step replays). The doc-block consequences list gained item (3) and the SQL carries an inline WHY comment; the -- MIGRATION-ACCRETION-EXEMPTION: marker is unchanged (the INSERT adds no CREATE/DROP so check_migration_accretion is unaffected).
fix-4.terminal_state_token_why: NOT 'superseded' (the token the engine uses on the supersession path at :14867/:14890). The `_fathomdb_projection_terminal` table (schema step 7) carries a STRUCTURAL `CHECK(state IN ('failed', 'up_to_date'))`, and record_projection_terminal / the backfill both use `INSERT OR IGNORE`. Under SQLite, OR IGNORE SKIPS a row that violates a CHECK and returns NO error — so a 'superseded' backfill would be SILENTLY DROPPED, no terminal recorded, and the cursor would STILL stall (a vacuous green). This is proven empirically by test fix4_superseded_token_is_rejected_by_check_so_or_ignore_drops_it (inserting 'superseded' via OR IGNORE leaves zero rows; 'up_to_date' persists). The only CHECK-valid, non-'failed' token is 'up_to_date', which honestly means 'nothing left to project here' for a deleted row and keeps projection_status out of the Failed arm (:12568 special-cases only 'failed'; advance_projection_cursor at :12102 only checks terminal EXISTENCE, not the state string). NOTE (out of scope, not fixed): the engine's own fix-32 'superseded' terminals at :14867/:14890 are therefore ALSO silently swallowed by the same OR-IGNORE+CHECK interaction — a latent P2 in the engine, distinct from this P1 finding; not addressed here.
fix-4.edge_node_cursor_disjointness_confirmed: write_cursor is globally unique per row from a single shared allocator; an edge cursor and a node cursor can never share a value. The backfill SELECTs write_cursor FROM canonical_edges only, so it inserts terminals exclusively for edge cursors and never fabricates one for a node cursor. Proven by fix4_backfill_touches_only_edge_cursors_not_node_cursors: an unprojected NODE at cursor 7 is NOT given a terminal by the edge backfill, while the dropped edge at cursor 5 is.
fix-4.fix1_fix4_complementary_confirmed: fix-1 (high-water reservation into RESERVED_WRITE_CURSOR_KEY, consequences item 2) solves cursor REUSE: dropping edge rows lowers load_next_cursor's MAX(write_cursor), so a freshly allocated cursor could COLLIDE with stale shadow rows below the old high-water mark. fix-4 (terminal backfill, consequences item 3) solves cursor STALL on the dropped cursors THEMSELVES. Both are retained; a comment in step 23 and doc item (3) state their complementarity for future readers. fix-4 does not disturb the fix-1 reservation INSERT.

commits.summary: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-schema/tests/tc33_fix4_projection_cursor_stall.rs — seeds a v22 DB with a body-bearing edge at cursor 5 (NO terminal = pending projection), a projected node at cursor 6 (terminal 'up_to_date'), projection watermark parked at 4; applies step 23; asserts a terminal now exists for cursor 5 and that a faithful replica of advance_projection_cursor walks 4→6 (past the dropped edge). Plus a disjointness test and the OR-IGNORE-swallows-'superseded' evidence test.
src/rust/crates/fathomdb-schema/src/lib.rs — step 23 backfills a terminal ('up_to_date') for every edge cursor before the DROP; doc-block consequences item (3) + inline WHY comment added.

fix-5.finding: strftime('%s', ?) was standing in as an ISO-8601 VALIDATOR, but SQLite's date parser is MORE LENIENT than the HITL-ratified hard-reject-ISO-8601 contract. Two confirmed leaks (both non-NULL, both the WRONG instant, both accepted before this fix): (1) WIRE boundary — iso8601_to_epoch_seconds (lib.rs :1823-1830) let bare numeric strings through: strftime('%s','2451545.0')=946728000 (SQLite reads the bare number as a Julian DAY = year 2000); strftime('%s','0') resolves to a pre-year-0000 epoch — so '0'/'2451545.0' were stored as unrelated instants despite the hard-reject contract. (2) GOVERNED i64 boundary — reject_unrenderable_edge_epoch (lib.rs :1887-1906) guarded on RENDERABILITY (does epoch_seconds_to_iso8601 return Some?), but strftime(...,'unixepoch') renders a below-MIN epoch like -62167219201 to a NON-NULL string '-001-12-31T23:59:59Z', so pre-year-0000 values (outside declared years 0000..=9999) slipped through.
fix-5.safety_context: Neither leak produces a NULL t_invalid, so the safety-critical 'no resurrection via NULL' property (HITL Note 1 / P3) was already intact. fix-5 is a correctness/contract-completion fix, NOT a safety fix — and it opens NO NULL path (verified: shape-gate rejection returns None which triggers the existing hard-reject; range-check rejection returns a typed InvalidArgument; NULL/absent still maps to Ok(None) = 'still valid').
fix-5.governed_surface_status: PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED — no AC minted; tracked by requirement TC-33 + test names. NEVER HITL-SIGNED.
fix-5.validator_choice: HAND-ROLLED byte/char shape validator, NO new dependency. Verified: the workspace has NO chrono/time crate, and `regex` v1.12.3 is present ONLY as a transitive dependency (via jsonschema -> fancy-regex/regex-automata); it is NOT a direct dependency of fathomdb-engine (confirmed against crates/fathomdb-engine/Cargo.toml — deps are fathomdb-embedder/-api/-query/-schema, rusqlite, jsonschema, serde_json, sha2, sqlite-vec). Adding regex to [dependencies] would be a NEW direct dep, violating the 'no new dependency' constraint — so is_iso8601_shape() is hand-rolled on ASCII bytes (u8::is_ascii_digit, which also rejects non-ASCII digit look-alikes).
fix-5.fix_a_wire_shape_gate: Added fn is_iso8601_shape(&str)->bool and call it FIRST in iso8601_to_epoch_seconds (returns None -> existing hard-reject fires). Accepts EXACTLY: YYYY-MM-DD (date only); optionally (T | single space) HH:MM:SS; optional .fff fractional; optional zone Z | ±HH:MM | ±HHMM. Rejects bare numbers, partial junk, whitespace, non-ASCII digits, trailing junk. The gate does NOT replace SQLite's calendar math: a shape-valid-but-impossible date (2025-13-45T00:00:00Z) still None-s out via strftime -> hard-reject (kept).
fix-5.fix_b_governed_range_check: reject_unrenderable_edge_epoch now rejects any non-null t_valid/t_invalid outside the inclusive numeric range [MIN_RENDERABLE_EPOCH, MAX_RENDERABLE_EPOCH], not renderability. Connection param dropped (predicate is now pure integer arithmetic); both call sites (validate_write Edge branch) updated. Typed EngineError::InvalidArgument still names the value + the bound.

min_max_renderable_epoch_confirmed.verification: Verified DIRECTLY against SQLite (bundled parser semantics): strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', -62167219200, 'unixepoch') = '0000-01-01T00:00:00Z' EXACTLY; strftime(..., 253402300799, ...) = '9999-12-31T23:59:59Z' EXACTLY; MIN-1 (-62167219201) renders NON-NULL '-001-12-31T23:59:59Z' (the leak) and MAX+1 (253402300800) renders NULL. fix-1 set these consts correctly (years 0000..=9999); NO correction needed.

fix-5.red_set: tc33_fix5_iso8601_shape_gate.rs — 8 tests: WIRE hard-reject via the fixture-keyed stub extractor (raw `SELECT count(*) FROM canonical_edges` == 0, not search): julian '2451545.0', bare '0'. GOVERNED i64 write of t_invalid=-62167219201 rejected + zero rows. Positive controls (gate not vacuous): '2025-03-20T09:30:00Z' (1742463000), '2025-03-20T09:30:00+00:00' (1742463000), date-only '2025-03-20' (1742428800), space-separated '2025-03-20 09:30:00' (1742463000) all still ACCEPTED at the correct epoch; MIN_RENDERABLE_EPOCH still storable. Added 6 stub-harness fixtures to tests/fixtures/slice15_byo_llm/fixture_result.json (append-only, existing fixtures byte-untouched: 174 insertions / 0 deletions).
